Lycopodium clavatum is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae, characterized by its unique, branching stems and spore-bearing cones.

Lycopodium clavatum has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and protect against oxidative stress.

The bioactive constituents of Lycopodium clavatum include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, including lycopolamine, lycosine, and lycopodium glycoside, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made from Lycopodium clavatum include tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
